prompt:
A prospect just replied to my connection with a very short message. What should I send back?

Conversation:
[Me, Day 0]: Hey James, thanks for connecting! It looks like you're scaling Inflect past the scrappy stage and into coordination territory which is where most of the interesting problems show up. What's the biggest friction point on your plate right now?

[James, Day 1]: Happy to connect Aleem. What do you do exactly?

Profile: James Tanaka, VP Operations at Inflect (40-person fintech infra startup, Series A, NYC).
assertions: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 1000 characters
Contains at least one Voss label
Contains a calibrated question starting with 'how' or 'what' — not a yes/no question
Output includes the meta-line with 'Phase:' and 'Tactic:' fields
Meta-line declares phase as Qualify (not Label, Proof, Pull, or Call)
Does not drop a quantified NexusPoint result (70%, 80%, hours-to-minutes, etc.) — too early
Does not ask for a call, Zoom, or meeting
Reply is short/medium, not a 4-paragraph pitch (they sent one line — Aleem should not respond with a wall of text)
